bead.title: Author docs/install/codex.md — OpenAI Codex CLI and Copilot Codex extension
bead.description:
Author docs/install/codex.md covering two distinct Codex surfaces and naming the difference clearly:

A) OpenAI Codex CLI (the newer agent CLI): the skill body and workflows catalog drop into the repo as referenced markdown; an AGENTS.md entry points Codex at the skill; invocation is natural language inside a Codex session.

B) GitHub Copilot Workspace / Codex extension: a more chat-style prompt-loading model; document how to load skills/helix/SKILL.md into the workspace prompt context or via Copilot's instruction-file mechanism. Likely lighter-touch than the CLI install.

Both surfaces share the same source content (skills/helix/ + workflows/). The doc must make clear that HELIX is not Codex-specific; the differences are purely in how each surface discovers and invokes a skill.

Sections:
1. Which Codex am I using? (decision matrix: CLI vs extension)
2. Install A — Codex CLI: file layout, AGENTS.md entry, verify snippet.
3. Install B — Codex extension / Copilot: prompt loading, repo-instruction file location, verify snippet.
4. Shared content reference: workflows/ catalog discovery rules.
5. Caveats: any feature parity gaps between the two surfaces.
bead.acceptance: docs/install/codex.md exists, names both Codex surfaces explicitly, and gives separate install steps for each. A 'Which Codex am I using?' decision matrix appears near the top. Both install paths share the same source content (skills/helix/ + workflows/) — no Codex-specific fork. The doc is reachable from the install README index.
